◆ __attribute__()

Definition at line 51 of file collapse_times.c.

114 {
115
116 /*
117 This routine computes the smallest non-negative solution of the 3rd
118 order equation for the ellipsoid, and corrects it to reproduce the
119 spherical collapse correctly.
120 */
121
122 /* Local variables declaration */
123 double ell;
124 double del = l1 + l2 + l3;
125 double det = l1 * l2 * l3;
126
127 /* Vanishing lambda1 eigenvalue case */
128 if (fabs(l1) < SMALL) {
129 ell = -0.1;
130 }
131 /* Not vanishing lambda1 eigenvalue case */
132 else {
133 double den = det / 126. + 5. * l1 * del * (del - l1) / 84.;
134 /* Check 1st perturbative order conditions */
135 if (fabs(den) < SMALL) {
136 if (fabs(del - l1) < SMALL) {
137 if (l1 > 0.0) {
138 /* Zel'dovich approximation */
139 ell = 1. / l1;
140 } else {
141 ell = -.1;
142 }
143 } else {
144 /* Check 2nd perturbative order conditions */
145 double dis = 7. * l1 * (l1 + 6. * del);
146 if (dis < 0.0) {
147 ell = -.1;
148 } else {
149 /* 2nd order solution */
150 ell = (7. * l1 - sqrt(dis)) / (3. * l1 * (l1 - del));
151 if (ell < 0.) {
152 ell = -.1;
153 }
154 }
155 }
156 } else {
157 /* 3rd order perturbative solution. For more details about the equations implemented, see Monaco 1996a */
158
159 /* Intermediate values */
160 double rden = 1.0 / den;
161 double a1 = 3. * l1 * (del - l1) / 14. * rden;
162 double a1_2 = a1 * a1;
163 double a2 = l1 * rden;
164 double a3 = -1.0 * rden;
165
166 /* The collapse time b_c will be a combination of R, Q, and D == den */
167 double q = (a1_2 - 3. * a2) / 9.;
168 double r = (2. * a1_2 * a1 - 9. * a1 * a2 + 27. * a3) / 54.;
169 double r_2_q_3 = r * r - q * q * q;
170
171 /* Check 3rd perturbative order conditions */
172
173 /* ---------------- Case 1 --------------- */
174 /* If R^2 - Q^2 > 0, which is valid for spherical and quasi-spherical perturbations */
175
176 /* 3rd order solution */
177 if (r_2_q_3 > 0) {
178 double fabs_r = fabs(r);
179 double sq = pow(sqrt(r_2_q_3) + fabs_r, 0.333333333333333);
180 ell = -fabs_r / r * (sq + q / sq) - a1 / 3.;
181 if (ell < 0.) {
182 ell = -.1;
183 }
184 }
185
186 /* ---------------- Case 2 --------------- */
187 /* The solution has to be chosen as the smallest non-negative one between s1, s2, and s3 */
188
189 /* 3rd order solution */
190 else {
191 double sq = 2 * sqrt(q);
192 double inv_3 = 1.0 / 3;
193 double t = acos(2 * r / q / sq);
194 double s1 = -sq * cos(t * inv_3) - a1 * inv_3;
195 double s2 = -sq * cos((t + 2. * PI) * inv_3) - a1 * inv_3;
196 double s3 = -sq * cos((t + 4. * PI) * inv_3) - a1 * inv_3;
197 if (s1 < 0.) {
198 s1 = 1.e10;
199 }
200 if (s2 < 0.) {
201 s2 = 1.e10;
202 }
203 if (s3 < 0.) {
204 s3 = 1.e10;
205 }
206 ell = (s1 < s2 ? s1 : s2);
207 ell = (s3 < ell ? s3 : ell);
208 if (ell == 1.e10) {
209 ell = -.1;
210 }
211 }
212 }
213 }
214
215 if (del > 0. && ell > 0.) {
216 double inv_del = 1.0 / del;
217 ell += -.364 * inv_del * exp(-6.5 * (l1 - l2) * inv_del - 2.8 * (l2 - l3) * inv_del);
218 }
219
220 return ell;
221}
SMALL
#define SMALL

◆ compute_collapse_times()

int compute_collapse_times ( int  ismooth)
inline

Definition at line 431 of file collapse_times.c.

431 {
432
433 /*---------------------DEBUG---------------------------------------*/
434
435#ifdef DEBUG
436
437 // Open a file for writing collapse times
438
439 FILE *collapseTimeFile = fopen("collapse_times.txt", "w");
440 if (collapseTimeFile == NULL) {
441 printf("Error opening file for writing.\n");
442 return 1;
443 }
444
445#endif
446
447 /*----------------------------------------------------------------*/
448
449 /* Local average and variance declaration */
450 double local_average, local_variance;
451
452 /* Initialization */
453 local_variance = 0.0;
454 local_average = 0.0;
455
456 /* Initialize Fmax, Rmax and velocity if it is the first smoothing
457 The initialization is done in a parallel way
458 Each thread initializes its corresponding array elements
459 This can improve the initialization performance when executed on systems with multiple processors or cores */
460
461 if (!ismooth)
462
463 /* Loop on all particles */
464 #pragma omp parallel for
465 for (int i = 0; i < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; i++){
466
467 /*----------- Common initialization ----------- */
468 products[i].Fmax = -10.0;
469 products[i].Rmax = -1;
470
471 /*----------- Zel'dovich case ----------- */
472 products[i].Vel[0] = 0.0;
473 products[i].Vel[1] = 0.0;
474 products[i].Vel[2] = 0.0;
475
476#ifdef TWO_LPT
477
478 products[i].Vel_2LPT[0] = 0.0;
479 products[i].Vel_2LPT[1] = 0.0;
480 products[i].Vel_2LPT[2] = 0.0;
481
482#ifdef THREE_LPT
483
484 products[i].Vel_3LPT_1[0] = 0.0;
485 products[i].Vel_3LPT_1[1] = 0.0;
486 products[i].Vel_3LPT_1[2] = 0.0;
487 products[i].Vel_3LPT_2[0] = 0.0;
488 products[i].Vel_3LPT_2[1] = 0.0;
489 products[i].Vel_3LPT_2[2] = 0.0;
490#endif
491#endif
492 }
493
494 /* Declaration and initialization of variables when the openmp option is ON
495 Inside the parallel region, each thread initializes its own local variables i.e.
496 mylocal_average, mylocal_variance, cputime_invcoll, and cputime_ell
497 These variables are private to each thread and are not shared among threads, ensuring data consistency.*/
498
499#if defined( _OPENMP )
500
501 double invcoll_update = 0, ell_update = 0;
502
503 #pragma omp parallel
504 {
505
506 /* Thread-specific variables declaration */
507 double mylocal_average, mylocal_variance;
508 double cputime_invcoll;
509
510 /* Support variables */
511 int fails = 0;
512 int pid = omp_get_thread_num();
513
514 /* Initializitation */
515 mylocal_average = 0;
516 mylocal_variance = 0;
517 cputime_invcoll = 0;
518 cputime_ell = 0;
519
520#else
521
522 /* If openmp is OFF
523 This approach is used to avoid conditional compilation directives within the code and maintain consistency in variable
524 names across the parallel and serial versions of the code.
525 When OpenMP is disabled, the program uses the same variable names, but they refer to the original variables directly */
526
527#define mylocal_average local_average
528#define mylocal_variance local_variance
529#define cputime_invcoll cputime.invcoll
530#define cputime_ell cputime.ell
531
532#endif
533
534/*----------------- Calculation of variance, avarage and collapse time ----------------*/
535
536 double tmp = MPI_Wtime();
537
538/* When you use a parallel region, OpenMP will automatically wait for all threads to finish before execution */
539/* If you do not need synchronization after the loop, you can disable it with nowait */
540#ifdef _OPENMP
541#pragma omp for nowait
542#endif
543
544 /* Loop on all particle */
545 for (int index = 0; index < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; index++){
546
547 /* Computation of second derivatives of the potential i.e. the gravity Hessian */
548 double diff_ten[6];
549 for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++){
550 diff_ten[i] = second_derivatives[0][i][index];
551 }
552
553 /* Computation of the variance of the linear density field */
554 double delta = diff_ten[0] + diff_ten[1] + diff_ten[2];
555 mylocal_average += delta;
556 mylocal_variance += delta*delta;
557
558 /* Computation of the collapse time */
559 double lambda1,lambda2,lambda3;
560 int fail;
561 double Fnew = inverse_collapse_time(ismooth, diff_ten, &lambda1, &lambda2, &lambda3, &fail);
562
563 /*---------------------------DEBUG----------------------------------*/
564
565#ifdef DEBUG
566
567 // fprintf(collapseTimeFile, "%d %d %f %f %f %g\n", index, ismooth, lambda1, lambda2, lambda3, Fnew);
568
569#endif
570
571 /*-----------------------------------------------------------------*/
572
573 /* Fail check during computation of the collapse time */
574 if (fail){
575 printf("ERROR on task %d: failure in inverse_collapse_time\n",ThisTask);
576 fflush(stdout);
577
578/* Workflow control */
579#if !defined( _OPENMP )
580 return 1;
581#else
582 fails = 1;
583#endif
584 }
585
586 /* Updating collapse time */
587 if (products[index].Fmax < Fnew){
588 products[index].Fmax = Fnew;
589 products[index].Rmax = ismooth;
590 }
591 }
592
593 /* CPU collapse time */
594 cputime_invcoll += MPI_Wtime() - tmp;
595
596
597 /* ------------------- Updates local variance and local average using OpenMP atomic operations ------------------*/
598 /* local_variance and local_average are shared variables that are updated by each thread (mylocal_variance and mylocal_average)*/
599
600#if defined( _OPENMP )
601
602 #pragma omp atomic
603 local_variance += mylocal_variance;
604
605 #pragma omp atomic
606 local_average += mylocal_average;
607
608 /* ------------------------------------ Updates total CPU collapse time -----------------------------------*/
609 /* cputime_invcoll and cputime_ell are local variables that are updated by each thread and then accumulated using atomic operations */
610
611 #pragma omp atomic
612 invcoll_update += cputime_invcoll;
613
614 #pragma omp atomic
615 ell_update += cputime_ell;
616
617#endif
618
619#if defined( _OPENMP )
620 }
621#endif
622
623 int all_fails = 0;
624
625 /* -------------------------- Updates cpu collapse time for a single threads -----------------------------*/
626
627#if defined (_OPENMP)
628
629 #pragma omp atomic
630 cputime.invcoll += invcoll_update/internal.nthreads_omp;
631
632 #pragma omp atomic
633 cputime.ell += ell_update/internal.nthreads_omp;
634
635 /* fails is a local variable that indicates the number of failures in the computation.
636 Each thread updates fails, and then the values are accumulated using atomic operations */
637
638 #pragma omp atomic
639 all_fails += fails;
640
641#endif
642
643 /* Fail check during computation of the inverse collapse time */
644 /* If there were failures, an error message is printed and the function returns 1 */
645 if (all_fails){
646 printf("ERROR on task %d: failure in inverse_collapse_time\n",ThisTask);
647 fflush(stdout);
648 return 1;
649 }
650
651 /* Calculating obtained variance and avarage */
652 double global_variance = 0.0;
653 double global_average = 0.0;
654
655 /* Calculates the global variance and average by reducing the values of local_variance and local_average across all MPI tasks */
656 MPI_Reduce(&local_variance, &global_variance, 1, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
657 MPI_Reduce(&local_average , &global_average , 1, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
658
659 /* If the current task is the root task (task 0), it divides the global variance and average by the total number of data points (MyGrids[0].Ntotal)
660 to obtain the average values per data point */
661 if (!ThisTask){
662 global_variance /= (double)MyGrids[0].Ntotal;
663 global_average /= (double)MyGrids[0].Ntotal;
664 }
665
666 /* Broadcasts the value of global_variance from the root task to all other tasks using MPI_Bcast */
667 MPI_Bcast(&global_variance, 1, MPI_DOUBLE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
668
669 /* Stores the true variance*/
670 Smoothing.TrueVariance[ismooth] = global_variance;
671
672 return 0;
673}

404 {
405
406#ifdef ELL_CLASSIC
407
408 double bc = ell_classic(ismooth, l1, l2, l3);
409
410 if (bc > 0.0) {
411 return 1. + InverseGrowingMode(bc, ismooth);
412 } else {
413 return 0.0;
414 }
415#endif
416
417#ifdef ELL_SNG
418
419 double bc = ell_sng(ismooth, l1, l2, l3);
420
421 if (bc > 0.0) {
422 return 1. / bc;
423 } else {
424 return 0.0;
425 }
426#endif
427}

Definition at line 315 of file collapse_times.c.

315 {
316
317 /* Needed variables for the integrations step */
318 double ode_param, hh = 1.e-6; // hh = initial step-size, ode_param = arbitrary parameters of the system == Smoothing_radius in our case
319
320 /* Setting integration time */
321 double amin = 1.e-5, amax = 5.0;
322 double mya = amin;
323
324 /* Step type: Explicit embedded Runge-Kutta-Fehlberg (4, 5) method */
325 const gsl_odeiv2_step_type *T = gsl_odeiv2_step_rkf45;
326
327 /* Newly allocated instance of a stepping function of type T for a system of dim = 9 */
328 gsl_odeiv2_step *ode_s = gsl_odeiv2_step_alloc(T,9);
329
330 /* The control function examines the proposed change to the solution produced by a stepping function and attempts to determine the optimal step-size for a user-specified level of error.
331 The standard control object is a four parameter heuristic based on absolute and relative errors eps_abs and eps_rel,and scaling factors a_y and a_dydt
332 for the system state y(t) and derivatives y'(t) respectively. */
333 gsl_odeiv2_control *ode_c = gsl_odeiv2_control_standard_new(1.0e-6, 1.0e-6, 1.0, 1.0);
334
335 /* The evolution function combines the results of a stepping function and control function to reliably advance the solution forward one step using an acceptable step-size.
336 This function returns a pointer to a newly allocated instance of an evolution function for a system of dim = 9 */
337 gsl_odeiv2_evolve *ode_e = gsl_odeiv2_evolve_alloc(9);
338
339 /* A system of equations is defined using the gsl_odeiv2_system datatype
340 This data type defines a general ODE system with arbitrary parameters. In this case we need the r.h.s of the system that will be solved: sng_system
341 The vector of derivatives elements: jac
342 The dimension of the system of equations: 9
343 A pointer to the arbitrary parameters of the system: (void*)&ode_param */
344 gsl_odeiv2_system ode_sys = {sng_system, jac, 9, (void*)&ode_param};
345
346/* GrowingMode is linear growing mode, interpolated on the grid. See cosmo.c for details (double GrowingMode(double z, double k))*/
347#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
348
349 double D_in = GrowingMode(1./amin-1.,params.k_for_GM/Smoothing.Radius[ismooth]*params.InterPartDist);
350
351#else
352
353 double D_in = GrowingMode(1./amin-1.,1./Smoothing.Radius[ismooth]);
354
355#endif
356
357 double y[9] = {l1*D_in, l2*D_in, l3*D_in,
358 l1*D_in/(l1*D_in - 1.), l2*D_in/(l2*D_in - 1.), l3*D_in/(l3*D_in - 1.),
359 l1*D_in, l2*D_in, l3*D_in};
360
361/* Assignment of ode_param in two different cases */
362#ifdef MOD_GRAV_FR
363
364 if (ismooth<Smoothing.Nsmooth-1)
365 {
366 ode_param = Smoothing.Radius[ismooth];
367 }
368 else
369 {
370 ode_param = Smoothing.Radius[ismooth-1];
371 }
372#endif
373
374 /*---------------- Integration step --------------- */
375 double olda = mya;
376 double oldlam = y[0];
377
378 while (mya < amax) {
379 /* gsl_odeiv2_evolve_apply advances the system from time t and position y using the stepping function selected before i.e. rkf45.
380 The new time and position are stored in t and y on output*/
381 int status = gsl_odeiv2_evolve_apply(ode_e, ode_c, ode_s, &ode_sys, &mya, amax, &hh, y);
382
383 /* Correct integration check */
384 if (status != GSL_SUCCESS) {
385 printf("ERROR on task %d: integration of cosmological quantities failed\n", ThisTask);
386 fflush(stdout);
387 return -1;
388 }
389
390 /* ---------------- Update ellipsoid axis --------------- */
391 if (y[0] >= 0.99999) {
392
393 return olda + (1. - oldlam) * (mya - olda) / (y[0] - oldlam);
394
395 }
396 }
397
398 /* In this case the ellipsoid does not collapse */
399 return 0;
400}

Definition at line 679 of file collapse_times.c.

679 {
680
681 /* Local variables declaration */
682 /* mu1, mu2 and mu3 are the principal invariants of the 3x3 tensor of second derivatives */
683 double mu1, mu2, mu3;
684 double dtensor[6] = { deformation_tensor[0], deformation_tensor[1] ,
685 deformation_tensor[2], deformation_tensor[3] ,
686 deformation_tensor[4], deformation_tensor[5] };
687 *fail = 0;
688
689 /* Performs diagonalization of the tensor by calculating the values of mu1, mu2, and mu3 */
690 mu1 = dtensor[0] + dtensor[1] + dtensor[2];
691 double mu1_2 = mu1 * mu1;
692 mu2 = 0.5 * mu1_2;
693
694 /* By define add[3] inside and outside the scope, the code ensures that any previous variable with the same name add is not affected
695 and a new variable add is created within the block. This is useful when the same variable name needs to be used again in the code without
696 conflicting with any previous variable of the same name */
697 {
698 double add[3];
699 add[0] = dtensor[0]*dtensor[0];
700 add[1] = dtensor[1]*dtensor[1];
701 add[2] = dtensor[2]*dtensor[2];
702 mu2 -= 0.5 * (add[0] + add[1] + add[2]);
703 }
704
705 /* Redefinition outside the block to calculate the squares of dtensor[3], dtensor[4], and dtensor[5].
706 This allows separate storage for these intermediate calculations, preventing any unintended interference or confusion between the two sets of calculations.*/
707 double add[3];
708 add[0] = dtensor[3]*dtensor[3];
709 add[1] = dtensor[4]*dtensor[4];
710 add[2] = dtensor[5]*dtensor[5];
711 mu2 -= add[0] + add[1] + add[2];
712
713 /* mu3 calculation */
714 mu3 = dtensor[0]*dtensor[1]*dtensor[2] +
715 2.*dtensor[3]*dtensor[4]*dtensor[5] -
716 dtensor[0]*add[2] -
717 dtensor[1]*add[1] -
718 dtensor[2]*add[0];
719
720 /* Check if the tensor is already diagonal */
721 double q;
722 q = ( mu1_2 - 3.0*mu2 ) /9.0;
723
724 if (q == 0.){
725 /* In this case the tensor is already diagonal */
726 *x1 = dtensor[0];
727 *x2 = dtensor[1];
728 *x3 = dtensor[2];
729 } else {
730 /* The solution has to be chosen as the smallest non-negative one between x1, x2 and x3 */
731 double r = -(2.*mu1_2*mu1 - 9.0*mu1*mu2 + 27.0*mu3)/54.;
732
733 /* Fail check */
734 if (q*q*q < r*r || q<0.0){
735 return -10.0;
736 }
737
738 /* Calculating x1, x2, x3 solution in the same way as it done in ell_classic */
739 double sq = 2 * sqrt(q);
740 double t = acos(2*r/q/sq);
741 double inv_3 = 1.0/3.0;
742 *x1 = -sq*cos(t * inv_3) + mu1 * inv_3;
743 *x2 = -sq*cos((t + 2.*PI) * inv_3 )+ mu1 * inv_3;
744 *x3 = -sq*cos((t + 4.*PI) * inv_3 )+ mu1 * inv_3;
745
746 }
747
748 /* Ordering and inverse collapse time */
749 ord(x1, x2, x3);
750
751 /* -------------------------------- Tabulated collapse time case -------------------------------- */
752
753#ifdef TABULATED_CT
754 double t = interpolate_collapse_time(ismooth,*x1,*x2,*x3);
755
756#ifdef DEBUG
757 double t2 = ell(ismooth,*x1,*x2,*x3);
758 if (t2 > 0.9){
759 ave_diff += t - t2;
760 var_diff += pow(t - t2, 2.0);
761 counter += 1;
762#ifdef PRINTJUNK
763 double ampl = sqrt(Smoothing.Variance[ismooth]);
764 fprintf(JUNK,"%d %f %f %f %f %f %f %20g %20g %20g\n",ismooth, (*x1 + *x2 + *x3)/ampl, (*x1 - *x2)/ampl, (*x2 - *x3)/ampl, *x1, *x2, *x3, t, t2, t - t2);
765#endif
766 }
767#endif
768
769#else
770 /* Final computation of collpse time*/
771 double t = ell(ismooth,*x1,*x2,*x3);
772
773#endif
774
775 return t;
776}
ord
void ord(double *restrict a, double *restrict b, double *restrict c)

1355

1361 *c = lo;
1362}

◆ sng_system()

int sng_system ( double  t,
const double  y[],
double  f[],
void *  sng_par 
)

Definition at line 239 of file collapse_times.c.

239 {
240
241 /* Local variables declaration */
242 int i,j;
243 double sum;
244
245 /* Needed cosmological parameters calculation at the given z */
246 double omegam = OmegaMatter(1. / t-1.); /* Cosmological mass density parameter as a function of redshift DIMENSIONLESS. From cosmo.c */
247 double omegal = OmegaLambda(1. / t-1.); /* Cosmological mass density parameter as a function of redshift DIMENSIONLESS. From cosmo.c */
248
249 /* y array contains the eigenvalues lambda_a, lambda_v and lambda_d */
250 /* In this case y[6] = lambda_d_1, y[7] = lambda_d_2, y[8] = lambda_d_3 */
251 double delta = y[6] + y[7] + y[8];
252
253 /* Calculating r.h.s sums of lambda_d_i evolution */
254 /* Loop from 0 -----> 3 beacause lambda_i has 3 component (it's the same for lambda_a and lambda_v) */
255
256 /* ---------------- Sum i != j --------------- */
257 for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
258 sum = 0.;
259 for (j = 0; j < 3; j++) {
260 if (i == j || y[i] == y[j]) {
261 continue;
262 } else {
263 sum += (y[j + 6] - y[i + 6]) * ((1. - y[i]) * (1. - y[i]) * (1. + y[i + 3]) -
264 (1. - y[j]) * (1. - y[j]) * (1. + y[j + 3])) /
265 ((1. - y[i]) * (1. - y[i]) - (1. - y[j]) * (1. - y[j]));
266 }
267 }
268
269 /* ---------------- Gathering r.h.s of lambda_a_i equation --------------- */
270 f[i] = (y[i + 3] * (y[i] - 1.0)) / t;
271
272 /* ---------------- Gathering r.h.s of lambda_v_i equation --------------- */
273 /* NOTE: here is the part where models of modified gravity can be introduced */
274 f[i + 3] = (0.5 * (y[i + 3] * (omegam - 2.0 * omegal - 2.0)
275
276#ifdef MOD_GRAV_FR
277 - 3.0 * omegam * y[i + 6] * (1. + ForceModification(*(double *)sng_par, t, delta))
278#else
279 - 3.0 * omegam * y[i + 6]
280#endif
281 - 2.0 * y[i + 3] * y[i + 3])) / t;
282
283 /* ---------------- Gathering r.h.s of lambda_d_i equation --------------- */
284 f[i + 6] = ((5. / 6. + y[i + 6]) *
285 ((3. + y[3] + y[4] + y[5]) - (1. + delta) / (2.5 + delta) * (y[3] + y[4] + y[5])) -
286 (2.5 + delta) * (1. + y[i + 3]) + sum) / t;
287 }
288
289 return GSL_SUCCESS;
290}
